meaning: adj.美国的,美洲的,哥伦布的n.[印]一种活字
examples: Recall the fate of the Columbian white-tailed deer, now in a protected status.
Partial remains of five mastodons, three Ice Age bison and a juvenile Columbian mammoth, as well as plant matter and insects, have been found.
In certain pre-Columbian Inca societies, groups were sent from the home territory to establish permanent satellite communities or colonies in distant tropical forests or coastal locations.
More recently, federal agents broke up a ring that was illegally importing questionable pre-Columbian artifacts.
The meal is a four-course affair, with a choice of British Columbian cabernet, merlot or chardonnay.
